evidence_quotes.quote: I respectfully ask that the Digital Fairness Act include protections for consumers who purchase digital video games.
Many publishers and platforms have started removing or disabling games that customers have already paid for, often with no warning, refund, or offline alternative.
online-only games that become unplayable when servers shut down
Video games are part of our shared digital culture and deserve the same legal protections as other digital products.
Consumers cannot lose access to purchased games without meaningful recourse.
Publishers provide refunds or alternative access if a game is removed or disabled.
Long-term access is prioritized through offline play, patches, or open server options, especially when a game has no ongoing commercial use.

paragraphs.text: I live in Norway, but i an not in the EU, but we have a EEA agreement. I respectfully ask that the Digital Fairness Act include protections for consumers who purchase digital video games. Many publishers and platforms have started removing or disabling games that customers have already paid for, often with no warning, refund, or offline alternative. This is deeply unfair and undermines trust in the digital marketplace. In recent years, over 100 digital games have been removed or made inaccessible, leaving paying customers with nothing. These include online-only games that become unplayable when servers shut down, or titles removed from stores so they can no longer be downloaded or preserved. There is growing public concern about this issue. The European Citizens Initiative Stop Killing Games has already gathered over 1 million EU citizens signatures, calling for legislation to prevent the destruction of purchased games and to protect digital ownership rights. I ask that the Act ensure: Consumers cannot lose access to purchased games without meaningful recourse. Publishers provide refunds or alternative access if a game is removed or disabled. Long-term access is prioritized through offline play, patches, or open server options, especially when a game has no ongoing commercial use. Video games are part of our shared digital culture and deserve the same legal protections as other digital products. Thank you for considering this important issue and the voice of over one million citizens.
